name: Pujol
description: Enrique Olvera's flagship restaurant, consistently ranked among the world's best. The mole madre — aged over 1,000 days — is the centerpiece. Tasting menu only, book well in advance.
address: Tennyson 133, Polanco, Miguel Hidalgo, 11550 Ciudad de México

name: Contramar
description: Roma Norte institution serving impeccable Mexican seafood since 1998. The tuna tostadas and red-and-green grilled fish are legendary. Loud, packed, and worth every minute of the wait.
address: Durango 200, Roma Norte, Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México

name: Quintonil
description: Jorge Vallejo's Polanco restaurant — ingredients-first Mexican cooking that feels contemporary without being gimmicky. The avocado tostada and ant larva dishes are extraordinary. Book alongside Pujol.
address: Newton 55, Polanco, Miguel Hidalgo, 11560 Ciudad de México

name: Maximo Bistrot
description: Eduardo García's Roma Norte bistro does French technique with Mexican produce better than almost anywhere. The menu changes daily based on the market. Relaxed room, serious food, reasonable prices. Book for dinner.
address: Tonalá 133, Roma Norte, Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México

name: El Cardenal
description: The definitive traditional Mexican breakfast in the Centro Histórico — hot chocolate, pan dulce, and the finest enchiladas in the city. Three floors of Talavera tiles, mariachis on weekends, and decades of regulars.
address: Palma 23, Centro Histórico, Cuauhtémoc, 06000 Ciudad de México

name: Rosetta
description: Elena Reygadas' Roma Norte restaurant in a restored Porfiriato mansion. Italian technique with Mexican ingredients done effortlessly. The bakery next door opens at 8am and is worth the detour alone.
address: Colima 166, Roma Norte, Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México

name: Licorería Limantour
description: Consistently one of the world's 50 best bars and the anchor of Roma Norte's drinking scene. Precise and creative mezcal and tequila cocktails. Come at 6pm to snag a table before the evening crowd arrives.
address: Álvaro Obregón 106, Roma Norte, Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México

name: Zócalo
description: The vast main plaza of Mexico City — one of the largest in the world, anchored by the Metropolitan Cathedral and the National Palace. Diego Rivera's murals inside the palace are free and unmissable. Best early morning before the crowds arrive.
address: Plaza de la Constitución, Centro Histórico, 06060 Ciudad de México

name: Chapultepec Park
description: One of the great urban parks of the Americas — 686 hectares of forest, lakes, and museums in the heart of the city. The hilltop Chapultepec Castle has the best panoramic view in CDMX. Walk the lake path on a Sunday morning when half the city is out.
address: Bosque de Chapultepec, Miguel Hidalgo, 11580 Ciudad de México

name: Palacio de Bellas Artes
description: Mexico City's most spectacular building — art nouveau exterior in white Carrara marble, art deco interior at jaw-dropping scale. Free to enter the lobby. Murals by Rivera, Orozco, and Siqueiros are among the greatest in the world.
address: Av. Juárez S/N, Centro Histórico, Cuauhtémoc, 06050 Ciudad de México

name: Xochimilco
description: The ancient floating gardens of the Aztecs — hire a trajinera for a few hours on the canals, bring drinks from the market, flag down the floating food boats, and let the mariachis find you. A Saturday afternoon classic.
